entry.title: Re: Setting up SAP PKI in BTP KYMA
entry.content:
I'm not sure. PKI services are handled internally by a dedicated team. Customers don't have access to it. 

Are you referring to key services from SAP Data Custodian?

entry.title: Re: How SAP SD module is implemented at Maritime Ports?
entry.content:
I am not sure if a simple answer can be provided to your question on a Public forum. 

How SAP is implemented? It will follow the selected Project methodology steps. 

In terms of which SAP Modules will be implemented, this depends on what part of the Business is to be covered on SAP, what SAP Suite offers and discussions between Solution Architects involved.

entry.title: Re: Outbound Communication to S4 On-Premise(OData) from B...
entry.content: And in the response in the backend you also see the 403 HTTP Code or is the call successful from backend perspective?

entry.title: Re: SXI_MONITOR: filter tested in SXMS_LMS_CONF, but not found by SXMS_EXTRACT_MESSAGES
entry.content:
I went through SXMS_EXTRACT_MESSAGES and found that type B filters only applied. That mean that I should set both check-boxes "Extract during message processing" and "Extract using external job" on Search Criteria for Selected Filter in SXMS_LMS_CONF.

But despite the fact that the filter is successfully tested and the index is updated, I could not get any message listed in output applying the filter in SXI_MONITOR.

entry.title: Re: Print Template in SAP Dm not visible

Still I cant see the template. I created a new Template without any space. 

KR,
sayuj.

entry.title: Validations for Postal code in Business partner
entry.content:

I'm looking for placeholder/link where I can see the validation logics that are in place for the Postal code in Business Partner.

entry.title: Re: SLOTTING PROCESS FOR SAP EWM
entry.content:
Hi!

Is it possible to trigger slotting during goods receipt or during delivery creation with a PPF action?
If it's possible, how did you set it up?

Or is it only possible via the report for slotting?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: In-House Repair in S/4HANA Service - Start with an Overview
entry.content:
Hi @Udo 

We are implmenting In-house repair scenario.

We need to know if exist, before the confirmation completed, a scenario in which a storage dispatches spare parts that are planned in service order?

For instance, a service order is planned in wich contains a service with spare parts, but the techician go to a storage in wich a person dispatch this spare part so as to later confirm with the service confirmation.

If it doesn´t exist this scenario, wich is the best way to do that?

We keep in touch wit "Advance Shipment functionality in Service Order" but this works for service order not for repair orders and works for consignment stock for customer.

We need to know if exist something like Advance shipment in service order for in-house repair.

Thanks so much for your collaboration.

Fernando

Thanks,

Selva

entry.title: In Cond Contract settlement process need to consider Contracts with PGI date GT settlement date
entry.content:
Experts,

Have a requirment in Settlement Management.

Current process:In condition contract settlement process via tcode WB2R_SV, currently system considers only the condition contracts whose PGI date is within the settlement date.

Expected :The system should als allow to settle the condition contracts whose PGI date is greater than settlement date.

Tried few BADIs with WB2_CC* but no luck. would be helpful if someone faced/implemented similar requirment.

entry.title: RAP Create button still enabled after disabling the feature
entry.content:
Hi !

I have created global feature with intention to disable CREATE action - it all works fine but instead of hiding cerate button it is still enabled and when i click it i get popup saying that create action is disabled - any advice on how to disable/hide the create button ?

SAPUI5 Version is 1.96.1
Here is my implementation of get global features:

 

 METHOD get_global_features.
    DATA lv_allowed_create TYPE boole_d.

    lv_allowed_create = zcl_qm_claim=>get_global_create_allowed( ).
    IF lv_allowed_create = abap_false.

      result-%create =  if_abap_behv=>fc-o-disabled.
    ENDIF.
    result-%update = if_abap_behv=>fc-o-enabled.
  ENDMETHOD.

entry.title: Re: LICKING THE GIF CYCLE
entry.content:
Link to the index page for gifsicle on NetBSD package source, for reference:

(old, out of service)

ftp://ftp.netbsd.org/pub/NetBSD/packages/pkgsrc/graphics/gifsicle/README.html

(new, working 2024)

https://ftp.netbsd.org/pub/pkgsrc/current/pkgsrc/graphics/gifsicle/index.html

entry.title: QM- Results recording
entry.content:
Dear Experts, 

I have a requirement from our internal customer and gain better understanding on how the data entry requirements are driven my the MIC Creation in the QM Module. Our production team has asked engineering to aid them in reducing the burden of entering MIC data during inspection of a device we manufacture inhouse. For this model line we have a shop order QTY of 1920 EA with a total of 12 MICs required over 23,000 check boxes of Pass or Fail to be entered per shop order, which run multiple times per week. The team is looking into what flexibility there may be in the QM system architecture that may allow them to simply ender ‘QTY Accepted’ and ‘QTY Rejected’ specifically for attributive (P/F) Functional Inspections, rather than using the auto generated 1,920 Pass or Fail boxes that populate when entering the MIC #. 

Any ideas are welcome. Simply put, I like to know is there any simpler way to minimize results recording for 1920 characters with ex. 1000 QTY Pass , 920 QTY Fails.

entry.title: Re: How do we confirm yield and scrap manually in workcen...
entry.content: Thank you so much! This really helps

entry.title: Re: Mass deletion of previous demand/requirements
entry.content: Thanks to Anup for this very helpful note!

entry.title: Blog Series- 1: SAP C4C Service cloud V2 integration with S4 Hana Utility (ISU)
entry.content:
Hello everyone, I am starting a new blog series for service v2 integration with S4 Hana Utility and in the very first part I am going to explain some of the key features of Service V2, pros and cons - V1 and V2 and Service v2 utility key functionality, Utility Iflows and synchronous webservice name

I am sure everyone is aware of the Buzz word “V2” and some of the Key features of V2, Listing down few of them here-

Key Features of V2 Service Cloud

  • Uninterrupted solution availability by leveraging modern technology and Hyperscaler experience.
  • API-first/microservices based services
  • Complement the core solution with tool sets to quickly deliver new composable apps
  • Almost Zero Downtime
  • Agent Desktop
  • Case management
  • Robust Global search
  • Native Email Management

[Will Publish another blog series for v2 features in detail]

Pros and Cons

V2 vs V1

Pro’s

Con’s

SAP Sales and Service Cloud V2

•  Microservices architecture – enables improved performance, scalability and flexibility.

•       Advanced business capabilities, including native intelligent sales and service solutions.

•       Latest version for Sales and Service – benefit from future innovations from SAP

•       Industry solution for utilities lacks some features available in V1. However, per SAP, it is expected to be at feature parity with V1 by the end of 2024.

SAP Sales and Service Cloud V1

•       Industry solution for utilities is mature relative to V2, and several large utility companies are already live on it.

•       Performance may be an issue while accessing information related to large customers/ accounts.

Disclaimer- This is entirely based on current V2 and V1 solution and solely my inputs. Both V2 and V1 is equally robust product.

Prerequisites for integration of Service cloud V2 with S4 Hana System

  • Software component IS-UT 808
  • OSS Note 3450264 needs to implement to get all new features of SAP Service Cloud Version 2 release.

SAP Service Cloud Version 2, Utilities Functionality 

Please refer the image below 

Service IFLOW Details

  Service Cloud V2 Integration with SAP S4 HANA Utilities consist of three different packages.

  1. SAP Service Cloud V2 for Utilities Master Data Integration with SAP S/4HANA
  2. SAP Service Cloud V2 for Utilities Premise Integration with SAP S/4HANA
  3. SAP Service Cloud V2 for Utilities Contract Account Integration with SAP S/4HANA

Master data Integration is same as V1 and there is no change in the Webservice while there is change in the contract account replication.

Webservice Name

Business partner- Same as V1

Contract account- CO_FKK_MDG_CONTRACT_ACCOUNT_BU

Premise - CO_UTILITIES_TECHNICAL_OBJEST

 Webservices for synchronous services are different from V1, and we need to use the V2 version for integrating with Service cloud v2. Please check SAP NOTE- 3253818 to see the complete list provided by SAP, which is having all the service details with the Integration flow Name, SAP Cloud Integration Source Path, Source System, Target System URL, S/4HANA Service Name 

Topic for Part 2- Some of the key difference with respect to Integration of V1 and V2 with S4 Hana system, Replication model, Details of the webservices Configuration and  etc*. Stay tuned to get further deep dive. Please provide your feedback and comments.

Thank you 

Chanchal Jha

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello David,

I am an SAP ABAP Developer with 9+ years of experience. I had to take multiple breaks in my career due to relocation along with my family across the globe. I am looking forward to bounce back in my career. As a woman, I believe I can get great opportunity if I can upskill myself. 

Thank you so much for this opportunity to apply for free certification attempt.

Regards,

Neethu Lohi

entry.title: Re: Issues faced in the Building Side-by-Side Extensions...
entry.content:
Hello  @Elroy , @Ivan-Mirisola,

could you please help me to find a solution within the same context: I am implementing Side-by-Side Extension on SAP BTP using CAP.

When trying to connect to the API for BusinessPartners entity I receive this error:

[cds] - Error: Request to remote service failed.
    at run (/home/user/projects/risk-management/node_modules/@sap/cds/libx/_runtime/remote/utils/client.js:234:31)
    at process.processTicksAndRejections (node:internal/process/task_queues:95:5)
    at async RemoteService.on_handler (/home/user/projects/risk-management/node_modules/@sap/cds/libx/_runtime/remote/Service.js:265:20)
    at async next (/home/user/projects/risk-management/node_modules/@sap/cds/lib/srv/srv-dispatch.js:68:17)
    at async RemoteService.handle (/home/user/projects/risk-management/node_modules/@sap/cds/lib/srv/srv-dispatch.js:66:10)
    at async RemoteService.handle (/home/user/projects/risk-management/node_modules/@sap/cds/libx/_runtime/remote/Service.js:292:22)
    at async ApplicationService.<anonymous> (/home/user/projects/risk-management/srv/risk-service.js:59:12)
    at async next (/home/user/projects/risk-management/node_modules/@sap/cds/lib/srv/srv-dispatch.js:68:17)
    at async ApplicationService.handle (/home/user/projects/risk-management/node_modules/@sap/cds/lib/srv/srv-dispatch.js:66:10)
    at async ApplicationService.handle (/home/user/projects/risk-management/node_modules/@sap/cds/libx/_runtime/common/Service.js:87:16) {
  statusCode: 502,
  reason: AggregateError [ECONNREFUSED]: 
      at internalConnectMultiple (node:net:1116:18)
      at afterConnectMultiple (node:net:1683:7)
      at TCPConnectWrap.callbackTrampoline (node:internal/async_hooks:130:17) {
    code: 'ECONNREFUSED',
    errors: [ [Error], [Error] ],
    message: 'Request to remote service failed.',
    config: {
      timeout: 10000,
      xsrfCookieName: 'XSRF-TOKEN',
      xsrfHeaderName: 'X-XSRF-TOKEN',
      maxContentLength: -1,
      maxBodyLength: -1,
      proxy: false,
      method: 'get',
      baseURL: 'http://localhost:39545/odata/v4/api-business-partner',
      url: "/A_BusinessPartner?$select=BusinessPartner,BusinessPartnerFullName&$orderby=BusinessPartner%20asc&$filter=LastName%20ne%20''%20and%20FirstName%20ne%20''&$top=1000",
      data: undefined
    },
    request: {
      method: 'GET',
      url: "http://localhost:39545/odata/v4/api-business-partner/A_BusinessPartner?$select=BusinessPartner,BusinessPartnerFullName&$orderby=BusinessPartner%20asc&$filter=LastName%20ne%20''%20and%20FirstName%20ne%20''&$top=1000",
      headers: [Object]
    },
    correlationId: '0777953d-190a-4db8-b39f-3def96db2075',
    toJSON: [Function (anonymous)]
  },
  id: '1899257',
  level: 'ERROR',
  timestamp: 1718637330838
}


The strange thing is that the cds is trying to get the API using this wrong url:

[cds] - connect to API_BUSINESS_PARTNER > odata { url: 'http://localhost:39545/odata/v4/api-business-partner' }

 

When the destination in package.json is set to the correct one:

 "cds": {
    "requires": {
      "API_BUSINESS_PARTNER": {
        "kind": "odata-v2",
        "model": "srv/external/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER",
        "credentials": {
          "url": "https://sandbox.api.sap.com/s4hanacloud/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER/"
        }
      }
    }
  },

Thank you in advance,

Best regards!

Hi @Johann 

Are the sessions in Walldorf offered only in German or (also) in English?

Regards,

Sven

entry.title: Explore cost-efficiently the latest SAP S/4HANA Build with Guided Beta SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02
entry.content:
What is Guided Beta Testing?

Guided Beta is a program that provides SAP customers with the opportunity to test the latest SAP S/4HANA build before its official release. During this testing phase, customers can explore the new features and capabilities of the latest version of S/4HANA in their own system landscape, using their own data.

The benefits of Guided Beta Testing are numerous.
Firstly, it enables businesses to explore new products and releases cost-efficiently before the official release.
Additionally, the program provides customers with the opportunity to test their individual processes with their data in their system landscape, reducing risk and saving costs and time.

Guided Beta Testing also allows organizations to expand their individual SAP knowledge and network. By participating in the program, businesses can gain a deeper understanding of the latest features and capabilities and connect with other SAP users to exchange ideas and best practices.

Guided Beta is:

  • A Proof of Concept with the latest non-released S/4HANA Build before Release-to-Customer.
  • Specific new feature(s) introduced with the latest release of S/4HANA will be tested. End to end Regression test scenarios cannot be supported due to the limited timeframe of the program.
  • With Direct Solution Management & Development Involvement and free of charge support from SAP.

Within the Beta phase, customers will get access to the prototype of the software components listed below, as well as the complementary documentation:

  • SAP S/4HANA PRIVATE CLOUD 2023 FPS02 (Only relevant for SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Customers)
  • SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02 (Only relevant for SAP S/4HANA On-Premise Customers)

Other common software components for both categories of customers include:

  • SAP S/4HANA FOUNDATION 2023 FPS02
  • ABAP PLATFORM 2023 SPS02
  • SAP FIORI FOR SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPSS02
  • SAP FIORI FES 2023 for S/4HANA SPS02
  • SL TOOLSET 1.0 SPS41
          - SUM 2.0 SP20
          - SWPM 2.0 SP18

What is required from the customer?

To participate in the Guided Beta program, customers must commit to the rules and regulations of the program by signing a Test and Evaluation Agreement (TEA). They must also involve their resources in testing and provide a test system landscape, including remote access. Customers must test their processes and provide valid feedback, as the program aims to improve the platform before its official release.

Guided Beta Testing is an excellent opportunity to stay ahead of the competition by exploring the latest SAP S/4HANA build before its official release. This program provides customers with the ability to test their individual processes and provide feedback, which can help SAP to improve and provide better services to its customers.

More details can be found in this presentation.

If you’re interested in joining the program, fill out the questionnaire and  apply before July 26th, 2024 at: Beta_S4HANA@sap.com

entry.title: How to enable PDF Preview in smart form print preview
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I can observe PDF Preview is disabled in my smart form output. Can some one help me to how i can i enable this option ? can we fix it via abap ?

entry.title: SAP B1 10 why items weight and size are mandatory?
entry.content:
On SAP B1 9.x we could create an item and not fill the weight and size.
With SAP B1 10, it looks like it is mandatory.

It is not convenient, so am I missing a configuration specification that would allow us to create an item with empty size and weight? or is it really mandatory?

entry.title: Mapping License with roles
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

We currently try to optimize the assigned named user license types for standard and custom roles and other authorization objects.

My current understanding already gives me the possibility to show me TCodes and the ACTVT corresponding to a certain role (AGR_1251 table), however when I now try to assign the most efficient license type (one that for example covers only the transactions granted to the role) either on the role or the used transactions, how could I get the information which license type covers which TCode / role from the SAP system?

As in the role-based user classification, I am able to see if a license covers a certain user with certain role, I would like to know if there is a way to understand which transactions (TCodes) or roles are covered by which license type.

In the rule-based user classification, one can define rules that provide you with a license type suitable for your rule set, but how is this matching established?

I would be grateful for any hints, no matter how far-fetched they are, whether it is a link between license types and standard roles, license types and transactions or even a link I am not aware of.

Thanks in advance and kind regards,

Johnny

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello,

I identify as a person in the below categories:

    • People in remote or underserved locations that may be not have access to the same economic opportunities as their urban or more privileged counterparts 
    • People looking to switch their careers mid-life

I have a background in business administration and have been working in this field for 5 years. Recently, I realized how important ERP systems are, and I’m really interested in becoming an ERP consultant. I have some experience using SAP in my current job, and it has sparked my interest in learning more about it.Switching careers mid-life can be challenging, but I am very motivated. My goal is to move from being an accountant to an ERP consultant, and I believe that gaining SAP certification will help me achieve this. This opportunity to learn and get certified in SAP is something I don’t want to miss this opportunity.

Thank you for providing us this opportunity.

Best regard,

Anh Duc Le

entry.title: SAP HANA Cloud, token expired
entry.content:
Hi All,

i'm working Business Application Studio for HANA Cloud, When i try to add a table in the Node, it is giving the below error.

Failed to update request header for header key authorization: failed to get cloud foundry token: could not retrieve the Cloud Foundry token: CF/XS CLI error (3001): cf command failed, reason=The token expired, was revoked, or the token ID is incorrect. Please log back in to re-authenticate

Could someone please help me.

entry.title: Creating a personal development environment #1 - Why Neovim?
entry.content:
In this blog post series I want to share my journey on how I created my personal development environment for developing cloud native SAP applications. The focus here lies in the term "journey", as I am nowhere near a finished state and still learning a lot along the way. This is not a tutorial about Neovim (others can do that better) - this is more of a documentation of my journey with a bit of focus on SAP specific things.

As most developers these days, I was using VS Code for a long time and was not really thinking about my development environment much. VS Code is a fantastic IDE (integrated development environment) and most of the features one could ask for available out of the box, such as syntax highlighting, language servers, an integrated terminal, and a wide range of available extensions. It was not until some of my programming heroes kept talking about neovim over and over again and how great it was, until I became curious. Neovim is a Vim-based text editor, and it was just amazing to see how fast and efficient these people where with it, mostly because of not using the mouse at all and the great customization options. TJ DeVries established the term "personal development environment", and I realized I hadn't personalized my previous IDE (VS Code) at all and had almost no clue about the available keyboard shortcuts. After all, the mouse was always right there so I was not forced to learn any keyboard shortcuts. So I realized to become fast and efficient I had to go down the Neovim rabbit hole - and here we are.

At first, I was very underwhelmed with the experience. After all, Neovim keybindings are very unique and unusual (from a modern perspective) and almost every feature has to be configured explicitly. A raw Neovim installation therefore is exactly that - very raw. But the plugin ecosystem for Neovim is fantastic and there are plenty of resources available to learn how to configure Neovim (see the end of this post). So I started doing my homework, hacking away, and configuring my dotfiles.

As you can see in the screen shot above, Neovim comes with a file explorer out of the box, but it doesn't look like a file explorer you know from other environments where it would show on the left of the screen and always be visible. I knew I wanted to recreate what I was used to and needed a plugin for that. The plugin ecosystem of Neovim is great and it is fairly easy to install them - not VS Code easy, but fairly easy.

You need a packager manager to install plugins/packages (I'll use these terms interchangeably) and a popular choice is lazy.nvim. In my central Neovim configuration directory (~/.config/neovim/), I created an init.lua file with following code to install lazy.nvim:

local lazypath = vim.fn.stdpath("data") .. "/lazy/lazy.nvim"

if not vim.loop.fs_stat(lazypath) then
  vim.fn.system({
    "git",
    "clone",
    "--filter=blob:none",
    "https://github.com/folke/lazy.nvim.git",
    "--branch=stable", -- latest stable release
    lazypath,
  })
end

vim.opt.rtp:prepend(lazypath)

-- this looks for all lua files in the lua/plugins/ directory
require("lazy").setup("plugins")

Ok, lazy.nvim is now installed and as you can see in the code, it apparently looks for a plugins/ directory to find all plugins. But let's take a step back and appreciate the fact that we are to write this code in Lua, which is a powerful, efficient, lightweight, embeddable scripting language. Its syntax is similar to JavaScript and it's very easy to use.

I was now ready to install my first plugin. I created a new file at ~/.config/nvim/lua/plugins/filetree.lua (the file name itself doesn't really matter) with the following content:

vim.g.loaded_netrw = 1
vim.g.loaded_netrwPlugin = 1

return {
    {
        "nvim-tree/nvim-tree.lua",
        config = function()
            require("nvim-tree").setup({
                -- call empty function to not apply default and interfere with telescope
                on_attach = function() end,
                git = {
                    ignore = false
                }
            })

            local api = require "nvim-tree.api"

            -- reimplement some of the default keybindings only for NvimTree files
            vim.api.nvim_create_autocmd("FileType", {
                pattern = { "NvimTree" },
                callback = function()
                    vim.keymap.set("n", "<Enter>", api.node.open.edit, { buffer = true })
                    vim.keymap.set("n", "fv", api.node.open.vertical, { buffer = true })
                    vim.keymap.set("n", "r", api.fs.rename, { buffer = true })
                    vim.keymap.set("n", "d", api.fs.remove, { buffer = true })
                    vim.keymap.set("n", "n", api.fs.create, { buffer = true })
                end
            })

            vim.keymap.set("n", "fe", ":NvimTreeFocus<Enter>")
        end
    },
    {
        "nvim-tree/nvim-web-devicons"
    }
}

This code disables the default netrw file explorer (lines 1 and 2) and then returns a lua table that lazy.nvim is on the lookout for. The nvim-tree plugin as well as the corresponding icons (nvim-web-devicons) get installed and while being at it, I also set up a few keybindings I want to use with nvim-tree. This is where the personalization and the quest for a "personal development environment" really starts. I decided to map rename, remove, and create to the first letters of the terms I associate them with, so r -> rename, d -> delete (remove), and n -> new (create).
I also decided to map more global operations that have to do with the file explorer to keybindings that start with the letter f (for file in my head). I use this same approach for other things like the language server and comments too, but we will get to that. This principle has really helped me learn and memorize the new keybindings fast. So to navigate to the file explorer is use fe -> file explorer and to open a file in a new vertical window I use fv -> file vertical.

This is what my Neovim looked like with nvim-tree file explorer opened. Still pretty rough, but it's a good starting point to continue personalizing it. We will continue with some miscellaneous configuration (for example a nice color scheme) in the next post.

Resources

  • typecraft on YouTube
  • DevOnDuty on YouTube

entry.title: Re: CloudEvents at SAP 🌁
entry.content: @Lidia-Sarasua, a public EDA strategy document is in the works, and that should help you highlight the differences and help decide which one to select.

entry.title: Re: CloudEvents at SAP 🌁
entry.content:
@BrianChanthat's a very good question.... deciding when to use data or notification events. As you correctly highlighted, there are performance and security considerations, and it might just end up depending on your needs. Security is a critical aspect, and in some platforms, it might be possible to establish ACLs, but then we add another layer of complexity there. I guess there is no right or wrong answer; sometimes a notification event might be enough. In other scenarios, the payload data might not be sensitive, and a data event will be great. Then there are times when you don't know when the event will be consumed and if it is not consumed in real-time the data might become stale/irrelevant if processed later.

There are many factors to take into consideration, and there might not be a straightforward answer to this dilemma.

entry.title: Re: Statement of Direction - SAP Data Services and SAP Information Steward, as of November 2023
entry.content: All of us - Data Services Consultants are worried about our careers due to vague and unclear information on future of Data services. Since 3 months, we have no relevant SAP certification to pursue and have no idea when will SAP think about us.. How could our certification was removed without a replacement in place? This is extremely disappointing from SAP. We can't loose our credibility, and SAP must think about thousands of Data Services consultants. Is this move done because SAP has decided to promote Syniti now?

entry.title: Output Parameter Determination for Production Order Output
entry.content:
Hi,

Upon accessing the manufacturing (production) order output thru Output Parameter Determination (OPD), it has Manufacturting_OP_Activity as one of the output type. However, when I tried to add getting an error message 'Selected output type MANUFACTURING_OP_ACTIVITY is not valid for this application'. 

Other than manufacturing order application, I could not find where Manufacturting_OP_Activity can be used.

Please provide how it can be utilized as an output for S/4 Public Cloud as customer has a requirement to print WIP label for a production order.

Thanks,

Falgun Shah

entry.title: Re: How to enable PDF Preview in smart form print preview
entry.content: My 2 cents: possibly, it's not available from the spool display, it's available only when the Smart Form is generated with print preview requested.

entry.title: Re: Spool file authority setting
entry.content:
Hello,

printing the device list with SPAD worked with the enhancement in GET_PRINT_PARAMETERS. I found the list with a filled authorization field in the spool output. 

Kind regards

Jan

entry.title: Re: Modify Z* Db table not working
entry.content:
Hi Raymond, I am using 'Commit work' if there is at least one update. Even if i don't use commit, its updating 1000's of recs.

What is LOB here?

entry.title: Re: SAP Green Token Launched as Generally Available!
entry.content:
What a great news.

thanks Gloria.

entry.title: Re: @odata.value always zero, is that possible to change it in custom handler?
entry.content:
Hi Shanshan, 
Hope you are doing well. I am facing the same problem. Did you solve it? if you did, would you please share the solution. Thanks

entry.title: A coding contest over coffee?!
entry.content:
Calling all developers! Why not take part in our latest contest on your next coffee break?! This is a coding challenge with a difference, since we'd like to hear about your most hilarious coding failure!

It's easy to participate, and you might even win a prize.

Every developer has had at least one "whoops" moment that they can recall and we would really love to hear about yours. Let OTHERS learn from YOUR mistakes 😃.

You can find all the necessary details and entries tab at:

Contest: My Most Hilarious Coding Failure

Good luck! And don't forget to tag your SAP Community friends using @.

entry.title: Re: SAP HANA Cloud, token expired
entry.content:
Could you please check if this help page gives you information you need: https://help.sap.com/docs/bas/sap-business-application-studio/connecting-to-cloud-foundry-using-identity-providers?q=authenticate&version=Cloud&locale=en-US

Regards.

entry.title: New to the community
entry.content:
Hello fellow members,

Although we (royal 'we') have been members of the Business Network for many years I am, personally, fairly new - and very pleased to introduce myself.

I lead the global commercial and marketing teams for a business specialising in the consolidation of subscription-based resources through the Business Network.

Along with my colleague and counterpart in Germany @AlexanderGraff, we enjoy working with many of the world's largest corporate businesses and intitutions.

I look forward to connecting and working with you.

Thanks,

JP Moulin

entry.title: Dispute Management: Change Reason code Dropdown based on Category
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We are having a requirement to dynamically change the reason code drop down(in t-codes SCASE/UDM_DISPUTE) based on the category being selected.

For example: if they select category A, the available values for reason should dynamically change based on a grouping logic.

We see an option in SPRO config to create an alternative input help, Finanacial Supply Chain Management -> Dispute Management -> Dispute Case Processing -> Attribute Profile -> Create Alternative Input Help for Attributes Kindly let us know how to make use of this and if any enhancement / BADI's are available to achieve this functionality.

Any feedback is greatly appreciated.

Thanks a lot.

Regards,

Prasad

entry.title: SAP CodeJam HANA ML In Mannheim 2024-06 Recap
entry.content:
At the beginning of June, we had the Getting Started with Machine Learning using SAP HANA and Python in Mannheim, Germany.

 It was kindly hosted by Brandeis Consulting and organized by @Jörg_Brandeis 

 @ChristophMorgen , who is the Product Manager of SAP HANA embedded ML, joined us.

It was great having Benedict Baur, who shared some of the examples from his book on the topic.

Special thanks to our chief chef Marco 👨‍🍳

A bit of networking, here I am with @DirkO , before the day continued...

 ...continued as the regular HANA Developers Mannheim with some great presentations, that you can read about in Jörg's post: https://www.brandeis.de/en/blog/events/htn-10-recap-en

entry.title: Sales order with purchase requisition, without special stock
entry.content:
Hi,

In my case, i need a sale order with purchese requisition, but does not have special stock.

I achived generate this sale order carrying out a transfer at the time of entry to free stock, but when generating the shipment, the system cannot capture the item that i have in free stock...

entry.title: Re: Develop an application utilizing the RAP Web API with SAP Build Apps framework in S4Hana OnPremi
entry.content:
Hi @anjan_paul  this is soo good... thanks for sharing your knowledge... 

Thanks-

Abhishek

entry.title: Gr processing time in Purchase order
entry.content:
Hi ,

I am using gr processing time to fake a Md04 report . We increase number of days and it does update MD04 . Now I have a requirement in which Gr processing time should be in negative. Is it possible in standard ?

entry.title: Business Catalog for SSCUI Activity
entry.content:
Hello,

I just would like to know which business catalog is necessary for Define Plant App in Business Process Configuration in SAP CBC. Where can I look up the required business catalog for missing authorization?

Thanks.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Business Catalog for SSCUI Activity
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

 

In the Project Experience, after searching for the Configuration activity you can see the ID number for this activity.

Having the ID number, you can find what catalog is needed for each configuration after downloading the Excel file Configuration apps from the Best Practices Explorer accelerators:

https://rapid.sap.com/bp/BP_CLD_ENTPR
*Please login to the Best Practices Explorer for being able to see all the accelerators.
 
After you search on IMG_Act BusinessCatalog Match sheet with the numeric ID of the configuration activity the business catalog will be displayed.
 
I have checked and the Business Catalog related to Define Plant activity is SAP_CA_BC_IC_LND_MM_IM0_PC.
 
You can check the SAP Note 3238660 - CBC- How to find the business catalog to access a SSCUI (Configuration Activity) and avoid authorization errors for screenshots and step by step in case you still have any doubts regarding the process to find the Business Catalog.
 
Kind regards.

entry.title: Re: i have a p user, how can i access SAP S4 Hana for pra...
entry.content:
Hello,

I hope you are well.

Try to follow the instructions provided in the post "Creating a P-User in SAP Cloud Platform to Practice SAP HANA".

I believe it will help you with instructions on this subject.

Kind regards,

entry.title: Re: Can you provide suggestions for migrating data from S...
entry.content:
Hello @KaviM,

The data migration in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition can only be achieved through the Migration Cockpit, hence no iFlows or custom programs can be used.

Please note that you can use an ETL tool (SAP or third-party) to fill the staging tables for both local and remote database approaches. However, if you would like to populate the data directly on database layer, you can use a remote SAP HANA database hosted on SAP BTP. 

For more detailed information: Migrate your Data to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Hope this helps.

Arturo Montes de Oca

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud - Activate / Deactivate
entry.content: We will be starting development on this in Q3! I am hopeful for a delivery in QRC4 for this for SCIM v1 and SCIM v2.

entry.title: Re: How to do Batch call in RAP Odata with CHANGE_SET ena...
entry.content:
Hi @Rajesh_Amirinen 

You may try to use concept #ISOLATION and #CHANGE_SET

Using #ISOLATION - You may allow process multiple data which are valid per your use case and show failed records as error... 

You may want to watch below reference videos, may help:

ABAP RAP CHANGE_SET 

ABAP RAP Isolation 

Hope this helps...

Thanks-

Abhishek

entry.title: Re: Costing sheet in networks and network activities
entry.content: Hi Ken, thank you for your help. However, in the costing variant I can only maintain one costing sheet. I have multiple costing sheets for the same network type. Is there a way the costing sheet can be copied from the WBS, or something similar? Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Unable to suppress group footer section based on cond...
entry.content:
It sounds like, currently, you have all 4 subreports placed in Group Footer 1 (GF1).
Right-click GF1 and select 'Insert Section Below'. That would result in GF1a and GF1b sections. Repeat twice more to get GF1c and GF1d as well.

Place 1st subreport in GF1a, 2nd subreport in GF1b, etc.

Use the dynamic 'Suppress' expression for each of these sections to Suppress the section when the current group value (what you call 'Header') does not match the one for that subreport.

For example, in the Suppress expression for GF1a, have something like:
---------------------------
{Header} <> "Header 1"
---------------------------

entry.title: Re: Assign multiple solution processes to one test case?
entry.content:
Hi, Ebba,
I just tried to create a Test Case to check this and only one Solution Process can be assigned to the Test Case.  Also checked after test case is saved whether I could add another and it seems limited to a single Solution Process.

Perhaps development could find some way to accommodate such a request:  

If you have a request for a feature/function that is not presently available, you can submit it at:

 - SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation  https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3501

Best regards,
Paula

entry.title: Re: SAP CodeJam HANA ML In Mannheim 2024-06 Recap

entry.title: Value in LC equal zero when selecting the special stock and movement type 601 in transaction MB51
entry.content:
Dear ALL, 

In MB51 report, for dropshipment purchase orders and deliveries, the outbound value of materials shows as zero. Ideally, it should reflect the purchase price. When we perform goods receipt (GR) for dropshipment purchase orders, Nessie places the stock under special stock  with the correct purchase price.
 
Please Why does the quantity in LC equal zero when selecting the special stock ?

María

entry.title: JSON model loading after the view is rendered, thus leaving the view blank
entry.content: I am facing a problem in sapui5 where my view is loading before the model maintained in the manifest.json is pulling data from the given json file and because of this the view is rendering blank. I have to refresh the page manually for view to wait for model to load before the view is rendered. I have used attachRequestCompleted function on the model to get model data but sometimes while navigating from one view to another I am facing this issue. I have also maintained preload: true for model in manifest. I can see the json file loading in the network tab. How can I solve this issue, does anyone have any idea?

entry.title: New Learning experience for mobile
entry.content: We have enabled the new ILX for learning in our testing environment. However, the mobile app does not reflect the new ILX mobile - is there something we are missing to get this turned on?

entry.title: $100 Off Temu Promo Code {acr897367} or {frg227060} First Order June 2024
entry.content:
Use $100 off Temu coupon code using the code {acr897367} or {frg227060} Working In 14 Countries To Recieve your discount coupon bundle. This Temu $100 Off code is specifically for new customers and can be redeemed to receive a $100 discount on your purchase.

The error "HTTP_MAX_CONN_EXCEEDED" in your GSTR1 RAP application in SAP S4 HANA Public Cloud is caused by the maximum number of HTTP client connections being exceeded. This occurs when multiple HTTP requests are generated for an instance and cannot be handled. To resolve this issue, you can try the following:

Reduce the Number of HTTP Requests:
Check if there are any unnecessary HTTP requests being generated in your application. Try to minimize the number of requests by optimizing your code or using more efficient methods.
Increase the Maximum Number of Connections:
You can increase the maximum number of HTTP client connections by configuring the max_connections parameter in your SAP system. However, be cautious when doing so, as it may impact system performance.

entry.title: Re: How to do Batch call in RAP Odata with CHANGE_SET ena...
entry.content: @AbhishekSharma, But I need this with create operation, change operation, and delete operation of the entities, not to specific action of the Business Object.

entry.content: If you are archiving a PO and get the error "Invoiced quantity not the same as GR quantity" what do you do with MR11 to get past the error message?  All supporting documents GRs and IRs have been archived.

The error "HTTP_MAX_CONN_EXCEEDED" in your GSTR1 RAP application in SAP S4 HANA Public Cloud is caused by the maximum number of HTTP client connections being exceeded. This occurs when multiple HTTP requests are generated for an instance and cannot be handled. To resolve this issue, you can try the following:

Reduce the Number of HTTP Requests:
Check if there are any unnecessary HTTP requests being generated in your application. Try to minimize the number of requests by optimizing your code or using more efficient methods.
Increase the Maximum Number of Connections:
You can increase the maximum number of HTTP client connections by configuring the max_connections parameter in your SAP system. However, be cautious when doing so, as it may impact system performance.

The error "HTTP_MAX_CONN_EXCEEDED" in your GSTR1 RAP application in SAP S4 HANA Public Cloud is caused by the maximum number of HTTP client connections being exceeded. This occurs when multiple HTTP requests are generated for an instance and cannot be handled. To resolve this issue, you can try the following:

Reduce the Number of HTTP Requests:
Check if there are any unnecessary HTTP requests being generated in your application. Try to minimize the number of requests by optimizing your code or using more efficient methods.
Increase the Maximum Number of Connections:
You can increase the maximum number of HTTP client connections by configuring the max_connections parameter in your SAP system. However, be cautious when doing so, as it may impact system performance.

entry.title: Vector Data Visualization and Comparision between TensorBoard and HANA DB
entry.content:
Objective

With the advent of Machine Learning and Natural Language Processing, word embeddings have become a fundamental tool. The first step of the transformer architecture is to tokenize the words and represent them as vectors in a high-dimensional space, which is accomplished by the embedding layer.

I was curious to visualize the vector representation and explore whether we could train a vector on a small dataset rather than using a large pre-trained model. Additionally, I want to explore the SAP HANA vector engine and use TensorBoard to visualize my own dataset. After multiple attempts, I found a way to satisfy my curiosity by combining the power of the SAP HANA vector engine and TensorBoard for visualizing my own dataset.

In this blog post, we will explore how to store vector data in SAP HANA DB, visualize the vectors using TensorBoard, and compare the Cosine similarity and Euclidean distances of a word in SAP HANA DB and TensorBoard.

Steps:

  1. Read content from file and generate word embeddings using Word2Vec
  2. Store the embeddings in HANA DB
  3. Visualize the embeddings in TensorBoard projector
  4. Compare the cosine and Euclidean distances of word vectors between HANA DB and TensorBoard

HANA Vector Engine:

To know about HANA Vector Engine refer these blogs,

  • First steps using the Hana Vector Engine with SAP GEN AI by @AronMac 
  • Practical introduction to SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine by @TiagoAlmeida 

Let's first create a database table WORD_EMBEDDINGS with a vector of dimension 100

 

CREATE COLUMN TABLE "WORD2VECTOR"."WORD_EMBEDDINGS"(
	"ID" INTEGER,
	"WORD" NVARCHAR(40),
	"VECTOR" REAL_VECTOR(100),
	PRIMARY KEY("ID")
)UNLOAD PRIORITY 5 AUTO MERGE;

 

The dataset is a text document prepared from https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-ai-core/sap-ai-core-service-guide/what-is-sap-ai-core.  

Generate word embeddings:

The dataset is trained on Word2Vec model which is designed to work at the word level within local contexts. The file content is preprocessed into passages or chunks before tokenizing them into words. This way we retain the contextual information within each passage while still adhering to the word-level training of Word2Vec. The embeddings learned will still be at the word level, capturing the relationships between words based on their co-occurrence.

We will use the Natural Language Toolkit libraries and Punkt sentence tokenizer for processing the text.

 

import hdbcli
import nltk
import string
import chardet

from gensim.models import Word2Vec
from nltk.tokenize import word_tokenize
from nltk.corpus import stopwords
from hdbcli import dbapi
from nltk.tokenize import sent_tokenize, word_tokenize

# Download the tokenizer from NLTK.
nltk.download('punkt')

 

Define function detect_encoding to read the raw bytes from the file and use chardet.detect to detect the encoding of the file.

 

def detect_encoding(file_path):
    with open(file_path, 'rb') as file:
        raw_data = file.read()
    result = chardet.detect(raw_data)
    return result['encoding']

 

Define function preprocess_text to tokenize the sentence into words and convert words to lowercase and remove punctuations

 

def preprocess_text(sentence):    
    words = word_tokenize(sentence)        
    words = [word.lower() for word in words if word.isalnum()]    
    return words

 

Define function process_file to process the content. The file content is split into sentences using sent_tokenize and each sentence is tokenized into words using word_tokenize. Splitting the file content into smaller units like sentences leads to more accurate and meaningful word embeddings.

 

def process_file(file_path):
    # Detect the file encoding
    encoding = detect_encoding(file_path)
    
    with open(file_path, 'r', encoding=encoding, errors='ignore') as file:
        content = file.read()
    # Split the content into sentences
    sentences = sent_tokenize(content)
    # Tokenize and preprocess each sentence
    tokenized_sentences = [preprocess_text(sentence) for sentence in sentences if isinstance(sentence, str)]

    return tokenized_sentences

 

 Let's start by processing the file content by converting it to lowercase, removing punctuations and tokenize the text by calling function process_file

 

# Path to the file
file_path = "C:/Users/Arun/tensorflow_datasets/Doc/SAPAICore.txt"

# Process the file
tokenized_sentences = process_file(file_path)

 

Next step to generate word embeddings using the Word2Vec model. Word2Vec has 2 algorithms, CBOW(default) and Skip-gram,

  1. Continuous Bag of Words (CBOW): Predicts a target word from the context of surrounding words.
  2. Skip-gram: Predicts the context words from a given target word.

To explicitly choose between CBOW and Skip-gram, you can use the sg parameter, sg=0 for CBOW (default).
sg=1 for Skip-gram.

 

# Train Word2Vec model
model = Word2Vec(tokenized_sentences, vector_size=100, window=5, min_count=1, workers=4)

 

The primary input for Word2Vec is a list of lists of words. Each inner list represents a sentence or a document.

  • tokenized_sentences - sentence used for training the model
  • vector_size - the dimensionality of the word vectors, it is set to 100.
  • window -  the maximum distance between the current and predicted word within a sentence, set to 5.
  • min_count -  ignores all words with a total frequency lower than this, set to 1.
  • workers - the number of worker threads to use for training.

Populate the HANA DB:

Connect to the HANA DB and store the word vectors into table WORD_EMBEDDINGS

 

conn = dbapi.connect(
    address='xxxxxxxxxxxx.hana.trial-us10.hanacloud.ondemand.com',
    port='443', 
    user='xxxxxx', 
    password='xxxxxxxx', 
    encrypt=True
    # sslValidateCertificate=True
)
cursor = conn.cursor()
words = list(model.wv.index_to_key)

for idx, word in enumerate(words):    
    vector = model.wv[word]
    vector_str = ','.join(map(str, vector))
    sql_command = '''INSERT INTO "DBADMIN"."EMBEDDINGS" ("ID","WORD","TOKEN") VALUES (?,?,TO_REAL_VECTOR(?))'''
    vector_str = '['+vector_str+']'
    val = (idx,word,vector_str)
    cursor.execute(sql_command,val)

connection.commit()
cursor.close()
connection.close()

 

Let's check the word embeddings in HANA DB. Table WORD_EMBEDDINGS contain 801 unique words with vector of dimension 100

Visualize the Embeddings in TensorBoard:

Next step is to visualize the embeddings in TensorBoard projector. To visualize the vector the embeddings are exported in a TSV file (embeddings.tsv) and the corresponding metadata (tokenized text) is saved in another TSV file (metadata.tsv).

 

import tensorflow as tf
import subprocess

# Create a directory to store TensorBoard logs
log_dir = 'log_Embeddings'
if not os.path.exists(log_dir):
    os.makedirs(log_dir)

# Save embeddings and metadata
with open(os.path.join(log_dir, 'metadata.tsv'), 'w') as metadata_file:
    for word in model.wv.index_to_key:
        metadata_file.write(f"{word}\n")

embedding_matrix = np.array([model.wv[word] for word in model.wv.index_to_key])
np.savetxt(os.path.join(log_dir, 'embeddings.tsv'), embedding_matrix, delimiter='\t')

 

The embeddings are loaded into a TensorFlow variable and saved as a checkpoint(embedding.ckpt).

 

# Create a TensorFlow variable for the embeddings
embedding_var = tf.Variable(embedding_matrix, name='word2vec_embeddings')

# Create a checkpoint to save the embeddings
checkpoint = tf.train.Checkpoint(embedding=embedding_var)
checkpoint.save(os.path.join(log_dir, 'embedding.ckpt'))

 

Embeddings, metadata and checkpoint files will be downloaded at the directory path

The TensorBoard projector is configured to visualize the embeddings. Configure the TensorFlow projector to visualize the embeddings using the metadata file. 

 

# Configure the projector
config = projector.ProjectorConfig()
embedding = config.embeddings.add()
embedding.tensor_name = embedding_var.name
embedding.metadata_path = os.path.join(log_dir, 'metadata.tsv')
projector.visualize_embeddings(log_dir, config)

 

TensorBoard is launched using subprocess to visualize the embeddings and metadata at default port 6006

 

tb_process = subprocess.Popen(["tensorboard", "--logdir", log_dir])
print("Access TensorBoard at http://localhost:6006/")

 

An alternate approach is to launch tensorflow projector and load the TSV file of vectors(embeddings.tsv) and metadata. TensorBoard shows the vector representation of 801 tokens in dimension 100.

Compare Results:

We have come to the final step, we will search for a word in the TensorBoard and in HANA Vector Engine and find its nearest tokens and compare the results. First, let's search the TensorBoard for the word 'dataset' applying Cosine similarity

Let's check the same using Euclidean distance

Now, let's run the same search in HANA Vector Engine using Cosine similarity

Search for word 'dataset' using Euclidean distance

Nearest points like 'alongside','additionally','xusaa','vs','operation' are present in TensorBoard and HANA Vector Engine though there are slight differences seen. This might be because of trivial variations in the implementation of Cosine similarity/Euclidean distance in TensorBoard and SAP HANA DB. For example, numerical precision, handling of zero vectors, or normalization methods could differ.

Conclusion:

In this blog post I've demonstrated how to generate word embeddings using Word2Vec, store these embeddings in SAP HANA DB, visualize them using TensorBoard, and compare the cosine and Euclidean distances of word vectors between SAP HANA Vector Engine and TensorBoard. This process provides valuable insights into the semantic relationships captured by word embeddings and showcases the powerful capabilities of SAP HANA DB and TensorBoard for handling and visualizing vector data.

Note: When importing Word2Vec from gensim you might encounter an error "ImportError: cannot import name 'triu' from 'scipy.linalg". To fix this error open matutils.py (will be stored at \AppData\Local\Programs\Python\Python312\Lib\site-packages\gensim\matutils.py) and import triu from numpy instead of scipy.linalg. 

Regards,

ArunKumar Balakrishnan

entry.title: Re: Unable to open Salesforce Database in Crystal Reports
entry.content: So, we've made progress but now I'm running into another issue. We were able to copy a registry file that included the database connector from my supervisor's machine. That was able to get us past the password part. But now, once it starts to load (at least it appears that it's getting past that) if freezes and says "not responding" and then the application crashes. It doesn't give me a crash report or anything. I installed my software on a different server, had to go through all the hoops again to load the database, same thing, the app ends up not responding and then crashes. Not sure what's going on now...

entry.title: How do I create an API in java
entry.content: I am developing an app using Java as a language and SQLServer as a database so I would like to know if it is possible and how to create an #API to integrate another app like Finance app into my application?

entry.title: Re: SAP Task Center setup for S/4HANA On-Premise
entry.content:
@saurkhare 

On investigation using report SEC_TRACE_ANALYZER , it was concluded that even though the destination was configured with Basic Authentication, the principal propagation flow was getting triggered on S/4HANA using the system certificate from Cloud Connector, resulting in a failing authentication on S/4HANA.

In the cloud connector, unchecking option "System Certificate for Logon" solved the issue. We recommend that you keep this option deactivated, as this lets you use principal propagation and basic authentication over the same access control entry, regardless of the logon order settings in the target system.

entry.title: Re: Migration Public Cloud for Low Value assets
entry.content:
Ensure you visit SSCUI Specify Amounts (102610) to check the amounts. 
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3e5fcf2c768746049b5627bd5a42f720/227f0f8f970a44f88d6c1b875971c306.html?locale=en-US

Read the migration protocol on the help guide https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3e5fcf2c768746049b5627bd5a42f720/a185c3d89dae4ffc85723f27f624944a.html?locale=en-US

And do a test run migration on your test system to ensure all data is good. And make sure you migration Group Currency or allow the system to translate it for you.

entry.title: Re: Takeover Values field is greyedout in AS91 tcode for...
entry.content: Check the capitalization date isn't after the legacy transfer date.

entry.title: Credit management with multiple rules
entry.content:
Hi team

On the Preparatory Activity and Migration of Customizing for Credit Management -- Define Settings for Credit Management Migration we have just one Rule for migration, what happened if we have more than 1 rule? how can we migrate customers to BP Credit role with correct rules if we can only select one on the prep steps.

What should I need to take care to perform migration for customers using different rules 

Regards

entry.title: Update Credit Group field
entry.content:
Hi team

Credit group was not updated during conversion to S4 HANA and is showed blank, we need to update it with some mass update. We have tried LSMW, Migration cockpit but is not working

Could you please advise how can we do it?

 

Grace

entry.title: Re: Submissions for "SAP HANA Cloud multi-model" developer challenge: Week 1 - Setup
DMEEX - Extended Data Medium Exchange Engine
entry.content:
Una de las herramientas que nos han ayudado mucho en el proceso de pagos para evitar hacer múltiples programas ABAP es el Motor de Intercambio de Datos Extendido

Extended Data Medium Exchange Engine (DMEEX) | SAP Help Portal

 

Esta herramienta nos permite definir cualquier layout de archivo que las instituciones bancarias nos requieren cuando necesitamos hacer la dispersión de pagos por transferencia bancaria a nuestros proveedores, ¡sin la necesidad de programar nada! Estos archivos de salida pueden generarse en formato TXT o XML.

 

Características importantes

  • El preconfigurado de caja de SAP ya trae múltiples formatos de diversos países, así no tendrás que empezar de cero. Toma uno parecido, cópialo y edítalo. (transacción DMEEX)
  • Puedes poner breakpoints o tracepoints para debuguear como se hace la generación del archivo DMEEX. (The DMEEX Trace Tool - SAP Community)

 

Formatos típicos

Usualmente aquí en México las instituciones bancarias manejan diversos formatos como:

  • Transferencias mismo banco terceros
  • Transferencias otros bancos (SPEI)
  • Transferencias con convenio CIE
  • Transferencias al extranjero

 

La plataforma Bancomer NET Cash es uno de los bancos que manejan estas opciones, entre otros en México.

Implementación o configuración

Ahora, ¿cómo hacemos la configuración de uno de estos formatos?

Primero revisa el requerimiento del banco y mapea cada campo requerido al campo que le corresponde de SAP. Dentro de la configuración del DMEEX puedes ver las tablas y campos disponibles para que puedas ir tomándolos de ahí.

 

Ya que tengas toda esta definición solo es ir acomodando los campos en el orden de salida requeridos y la longitud indicada de cada campo.

Ejemplo

 

 

Opciones en el mapeo de campos

Afortunadamente la herramienta cuenta con diversas formas de mapear campos, no todos tienen que venir de un campo que exista en una de las tablas disponibles del programa de pagos, también puedes poner datos fijos, puedes definir llenar un campo si cumple con ciertas condiciones, o bien si ya de plano no se encuentra el dato puedes agregar código ABAP para completar el requerimiento.

 

No necesitas crear vías de pago para cada tipo de archivo de salida, solo considera que condiciones se deben cumplir para determinar que es un pago a un mismo banco, o a un banco tercero o a un banco extranjero o un convenio CIE, etc. Puedes hacer las definiciones adecuadas para que el DMEE "piense" y decida que layout formar de salida.

 

Ejemplos

En las imágenes puedes ver estos ejemplos:

  1. Puedes ver las condiciones para determinar que la salida es un pago a tercero con cuenta Bancomer.
    1. La condición dice que si el banco destino es Bancomer (FPAYH-ZBNKY='012') y si no trae referencia CIE (FPAYP-STRFR='')

2. Puedes ver la determinación de un campo fijo, ahí se indica la longitud del campo y el valor constante que va a tomar. El campo es CVEOPE, con una longitud de 3 y con valor constante de "PTC"

3. Puedes ver la determinación de un campo en función de una tabla, también el formato de salida del campo, por ejemplo, para el campo Cuenta cargo dice C0 - significa alineado a la derecha rellenado con ceros a la izquierda y su fuente es FPAYHX-UBKNT.

4. Si tu archivo de salida tiene una línea cabecera y otra de detalle, esto lo puedes hacer con el uso de los niveles. En cada nivel correspondiente vas poniendo los campos que se utilizan ahí, y también indicas como saber cuál es el nivel detalle, por ejemplo, en la imagen se ve que el detalle es el nivel 3 y se genera un registro en la salida como detalla por cada documento contable de pago generado. FPAYH-DOC1R.

 

Finalmente

Finalmente te comento que la aplicación fiori donde puedes descargar el archivo luego de ejecutar y contabilizar el programa de pagos se llama Gestionar medios de pago. Al ingresar a esta aplicación puedes colocar los parámetros de filtro que son la sociedad, la fecha de ejecución y el identificador de ejecución de la propuesta de pagos, tal como se ve en la imagen anexa, podrás ver la ejecución realizada lista para seleccionarla y solo darle click en Descargar. En esa lista puedes ver también el nombre del formato de salida creado en el DMEEX.

 

 

Espero te sirva esta información para que puedas hacer uso de esta importante herramienta y maximices el uso de SAP.

 

"El éxito es la suma de pequeños esfuerzos repetidos, un día sí y al otro también" - Robert Collier

entry.title: Re: Depreciation below zero
entry.content:
I think you could only achieve this by posting depreciation manually. 

What is the business rationale for NBV to be less than zero?

entry.title: Re: Asset Depreciation value changed in the middle of the...
entry.content:
Is there more than one phase in the depreciation key?
Was there subsequent transaction posted to the asset? 

You should check the depreciation calculation in the asset master data. AW01N for OP, of Manage Fixed Assets for S/4Hana Cloud.

entry.title: certification check for an operation activity in Order pod (assigning to phase)
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

Is there any possibility to assign "certification check" to an operation activity in order pod (assigning certification to a phase).

Thanks in advance,

regards,

Anvesh

entry.title: SAPRouter connection for RISE
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

There a possibility to connect a local SAPRouter to a S/4 HANA system on RISE? We need to reach that system for the integration with a Payroll System.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: SAP Sustainability Control Tower – Uploading and showing Data in Fiori Tiles– System Setup
entry.content: Sustainability Metrics catalog is no longer in SCT, I see two apps "Manage metrics" and "Manage ESG Data". Can you please elaborate on the process to upload GHG data? I am using "Manage ESG data" to upload "Injury" data and getting the following error,

entry.title: Re: CBTA using dynamic date parameter input #solman
entry.content:
I have a question I hope someone can help me with:

Why is the token/code returning the date format DDMMYYYY without the zero before the single digit month; for example, instead of returning 06/13/2024 it's returning 6/13/2024?

Here are the details of this issue:

  1. The CBTA script extracts data from SAP, creates a file and sends the resulting file to an AL11 directory.
  2. The script navigates to the directory path, sorts the files in descending order such that the current date displays on the top row.
  3. Then the date is captured with checkproperty (a parameter) and compared to today's date, which is picked up as it is displayed on screen (format MMDDYYYY) as expected.
  4. However, when the parameter is compared to today's date using any of the three methods listed in item 5 below; the date does not include the zero before the month, where month is June for instance.  For example, Jun is is returned as 6/13/2024 (format MDDYYYY) instead of 06/13/2024.  Therefore, the script fails with the following error:     

      Target: GridCell_0_MOD_DATE
     - The 'GetCellValue(0, "MOD_DATE")' property is set to: 06/13/2024
      - It has been compared to: 6/13/2024
     - Using the operator: =

 

    5. Received the same error using any/all of the 3 items below:

  • %today%
  • %=month(now())&"/"&day(now())+1&"/"&year(now())%
  • %=Month($today$)%/%=Day($today$)%/%=Year($today$)%

Thank you,

Jeannine

entry.title: Payroll connection to S/4HANA RISE
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have a EC Payroll System and a S/4HANA system on RISE we want to connect and integrate both systems but because the S/4 HANA is a private system the RFC connections to that system doesnt work for remote systems so how we can connect the systems between? The S/4 can reach the payroll system by a SAPRouter but the EC Payroll cant reach the S/4 System. We can create a SR to open the connection to RISE? Or maybe we need another way to success the connection?

Thanks

How can I write this in my amdp select

entry.title: Basic auth in API Manager: client + user
entry.content:
Hello,

I need to set up the basic authentication in the API Management. I need it to check both the username&password + the cliend_id&client_secret passed on by the caller. If the user or the client_id is missing, the auth must fail. I managed to get them work but not together.

How is this possible to achieve in the API Management?

Cristian

entry.title: Mass update of Credit Group Field
entry.content:
All- Please we need advise on the best approach to update the Credit field(Credit Group) in S/4 HANA below.  Any help you can offer will be greatly appreciated. We have tried the following mass update transaction, but none of it works for the Credit Group

Transactions

UKM_MASSDATA_UPDATE

UKM_MASS_UPD1 for Mass Change to External Credit Info.
UKM_MASS_UPD2 for Mass Change to Score

UKM_MASS_UPD3 for Mass Change to Credit Limit
UKM_MASS_UPD4 for Mass Change to Check Rule
UKM_MASS_UPD5 for Assigning UKM000 Role

 

 

 

 

Thanks,

entry.title: Re: Submissions for "SAP HANA Cloud multi-model" developer challenge: Week 2 - Words as Ve
Re: Submissions for "SAP HANA Cloud multi-model" developer challenge: Week 2 - Words as Ve
entry.content:
Hi Vitaliy, all worked fine, also with using 500000 words, also interesting: showing more than LIMIT 1 results.

A little bit stranger:

word1='Munich'

related_word1='Oktoberfest'

word2='Stuttgart'

(It is not "Septemberfest", but "Cannstatter Wwasen" - but it usually takes playe in september)


Experimenting with capitals worked for Brasil, Braslilia -> Australia, Canberra, but not for Suisse, Bern.

entry.title: 4RF - Project Control - Resourcing and Procurement in relation to/with Q stock in STO's
entry.content:
Our question is: can we  move Special Stock Q from one Plant/Company Code to another Plant/Company Code?

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: 4RF - Project Control - Resourcing and Procurement in...
entry.content:
Yes, its possible to create an STO / Delivery with account assignment to a WBS Element. Where you can transfer from a WBS Element in plant A to the same WBS Element in Plant B via the STO process. (Currently it is only possible to provide one WBS element in STO)

When the STO is created they enter the WBS. The Outbound Delivery is created for the same WBS. They post the 641Q and the 101Q and both refer to the same WBS Element

KBA 2628973 - Stock Transport Order between different Sales Orders / WBS Elements.

If you want an issuing from one wbs and then receive into a second WBS Element (2 separate wbs elements)

Since the WBS Element needs to be specified in the Stock Transport Order and we only have 1 field for a single WBS Element, it is not possible to specify an issuing WBS Element different than a receiving WBS Element. We just move the stock of the project assigned to the WBS Element between two plants.

A workaround could be to use 'Post Goods Movement' (MIGO) here it is possible to make a transfer posting with the movement type 415 Q from here you can then specify two different WBS Elements in the transfer. However the 415Q posting is not part of the STO / Delivery process – its completely separate so as a result there is no Billing Documents / Invoicing for the 415Q posting.

entry.title: Re: How To Upload Data to a Public or Private Planning Version Using a File Upload Custom Widget Wi
entry.content: @thayumanavanThanks for the feedback. I implemented an additional model and changed the import to this. While designing the cross model copy rules it seems that also here hierarchy nodes are not allowed. Do I miss any general setting?

entry.title: How to define accounts for COGS?
entry.content:
We would like to know how to check/define the accounts used for splitting the costs of good sold.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to define accounts for COGS?
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,


A cost splitting profile is predelivered by SAP and can be adapted to your requirements using the configuration activity Define Accounts for Splitting the Cost of Goods Sold. Only if the source and target accounts are defined can a COGS split take place. For more information, see the respective configuration help in your system and the following product assistance:

  • Splitting the Cost of Goods Sold

  • Manage COGS Splits

  • Reposting COGS Split

Furthermore, you can also refer to the below Blog Post for details:

Efficiently Splitting the Cost of Goods Sold with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Cloud


Best Regards,
SAP Support
